tool_input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this directory is

`<HOME>/Projects` is `$PROJECTS_DIR` — a **workspace root**, not a single codebase. It holds ~50 independent repositories spanning Go, Python, TypeScript/Obsidian plugins, Ansible, Hugo, Rust, and personal notes. The directory itself is not a Git repo (only a `.gitignore` excluding `dist/`).

`PROJECTS_DIR` is set by the active `devbox-setup` profile: `personal` → `~/Projects`, `work` → `~/Work`. The `proj` and `wt` tooling refuses to run outside it.

Treat each immediate subdirectory as its own project with its own toolchain. **Do not** create code, configs, or shared abstractions at this level.

## Project layout: two styles

Two layouts coexist here. They behave differently and matter when you orient inside a project.

### New-style (preferred)

```
<project>/
├── CLAUDE.md              # project-specific, shared across base/ and all worktrees
├── .claude/               # project-specific Claude config (workflow.json, agents, etc.)
└── base/                  # the actual git repository (main/master checkout)
    ├── .git/
    └── …source…
└── <branch-1>/            # worktree, sibling of base/
└── <branch-2>/            # worktree, sibling of base/
```

- The git repository lives in `base/`. The wrapper directory is **not** a git repo.
- `CLAUDE.md` and `.claude/` are intentionally kept at wrapper level so they apply to every worktree without duplication.
- Worktrees are siblings of `base/`, one directory per branch. Branch names with slashes are normalised: `feature/login` → `<project>/feature-login/`.

Current new-style projects: `ai-assistant-playground`, `career-story`, `go-core`, `go-skeleton`, `llm-orchestrator`, `llm-tools-configuration`, `local-telemetry-server`, `md-printer`, `personal-notes`, `python-skeleton`, `warp-and-weft`.

### Old-style (legacy, still fine)

```
<project>/
├── .git/                  # repo at wrapper level
├── CLAUDE.md
└── …source…
```

- Single working tree, no worktree workflow available.
- `proj <name>` cd's straight in and prints a warning suggesting `proj convert <name>` to migrate. Migration is opt-in — running an old-style project as-is is fully supported.

Current old-style projects: `a<HOME>.github.io`, `agentic-rag-mcp`, `cv`, `devbox-setup`, `docker-protobuf`, `everything-claude-code`, `gdt`, `go-devtools`, `go-versioning-helper`, `hit-blog`, `ka<HOME>_notes`, `obsidian-content-plugin`, `obsidian-tasks-system`, `proto-store`, `provider`, `rules_proto`, `rust-playground`.

## The `proj` and `wt` commands

Both are fish functions installed by `devbox-setup` (`roles/devbox/files/.config/fish/functions/{proj,wt}.fish`). `wt` is a thin alias for `proj wt`. The README of `devbox-setup` is the authoritative reference; quick map below.

### `proj` — project navigation and lifecycle

| Command | Behaviour |
|---------|-----------|
| `proj <name>` | cd into `<project>/base` if new-style; cd into `<project>` and warn if old-style |
| `proj ls` | list every directory under `$PROJECTS_DIR` |
| `proj clone <url>` | clone into `<project>/base/`, install hooks, cd in |
| `proj new <name>` | create `<project>/base/` + `git init`, install hooks, cd in |
| `proj convert <name>` | migrate old-style → new-style, preserving root `CLAUDE.md` / `.claude/` at wrapper level |
| `proj hooks` | re-install git hooks into current project's `base/` |

### `wt` — worktree management (only on new-style projects)

| Command | Behaviour |
|---------|-----------|
| `wt add <branch> [--from <base-branch>]` | create worktree at `<project>/<branch>/`; if branch exists locally → check out; if remote-only → track; else create new from default branch |
| `wt fork <branch>` | new worktree forked from current HEAD |
| `wt sync` | merge detected upstream into current worktree |
| `wt push` | `git push -u origin <current-branch>` |
| `wt ls` / `wt status` | list worktrees; `status` adds ahead-count + `gh pr view` state |
| `wt rm [-f] <name>` | remove worktree and delete branch (uses `-d`, `-f` upgrades to `-D`) |
| `wt clean` | remove all fully-merged worktrees |
| `wt <name>` | cd to worktree directory |

### `.wtfiles` manifest

When a project's `base/` contains `.wtfiles`, `wt add` and `wt fork` use it to seed the new worktree with gitignored paths (e.g. `.env`, local config). Verbs: `link` (default symlink), `copy` (per-worktree divergence). Without a manifest, the user gets an interactive prompt.

### Global rule reminder

The User Authority Protocol forbids using `EnterWorktree` directly. Always go through `wt add` (or `claude --worktree` from outside Claude Code, which delegates via hook).

## Cross-cutting

- The user's global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (User Authority Protocol, approval gates, language policy, Go formatting policy, agent workflow opt-in) applies everywhere in this workspace and overrides any project-level convention.
- Projects opt into the mandatory agent pipeline by adding `.claude/workflow.json`. Currently `agentic-rag-mcp` and `ai-assistant-playground` are opted in. Without `workflow.json`, direct edits are allowed subject to the global protocol.
- `projects.tar.bz2` at the workspace root is a snapshot archive — leave it alone unless explicitly asked.

## What not to do at this level

- Do not run `/init`, `wt`, or `proj wt` against the workspace root — they require a project subdirectory.
- Do not write code, configs, scripts, or `.claude/` content directly at `<HOME>/Projects/`.
- Do not assume tooling from one project applies to another — manifests and Makefiles are per-project.

tool_response.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this directory is

`<HOME>/Projects` is a **workspace root**, not a single codebase. It holds ~50 independent repositories spanning Go services, Python tools, TypeScript/Obsidian plugins, Ansible roles, a Hugo blog, and personal notes. The directory itself is not a Git repo (it only has a `.gitignore` excluding `dist/`).

Treat each immediate subdirectory as its own project with its own toolchain, conventions, and lifecycle. Do **not** create code, configs, or shared abstractions at this level.

## Working inside a project

Always change scope into the project subdirectory before doing real work. Each project owns its own:

- Build/test/lint commands (`Makefile`, `package.json`, `go.mod`, `pyproject.toml`, `ansible.cfg`, …)
- `.claude/` configuration (when present)
- `CLAUDE.md` with project-specific guidance

When entering a project, read its `CLAUDE.md` first if one exists. The following projects already have one and should be considered authoritative for their respective scopes:

- `agentic-rag-mcp/` — Go MCP server for retrieval-augmented generation
- `codenavd/` — code navigation service
- `devbox-setup/` — Ansible-driven dev environment (deploys this very `~/.claude` config)
- `go-versioning-helper/` — Go CLI for release versioning
- `obsidian-tasks-system/` — TypeScript Obsidian plugin
- `proto-store/` — Go protobuf storage service
- `provider/` — Go library (cheap/expensive provider pattern)
- `warp-and-weft/` — personal notes/principles repo

Projects with `.claude/workflow.json` (currently `agentic-rag-mcp`, `ai-assistant-playground`) opt into the mandatory agent pipeline — code edits there MUST go through `/implement`. Projects without one allow direct edits subject to the global User Authority Protocol.

## Worktree layout convention

Projects that use Git worktrees follow a strict sibling layout (see `warp-and-weft/`, `llm-orchestrator/`):

```
Projects/<project>/base/        # primary checkout (default branch)
Projects/<project>/<branch>/    # additional worktrees, one per branch
```

Create worktrees via `proj wt add <branch>` (devbox-provided CLI) or `claude --worktree`, **never** via raw `git worktree add` or `EnterWorktree`. When operating inside `<project>/<branch>/`, treat that worktree as the working copy — the project's tooling expects it.

## Language mix at a glance

When orienting in an unfamiliar project, the top-level manifest tells you which toolchain applies:

| Manifest | Toolchain |
|----------|-----------|
| `go.mod` | Go — use `goimports -local <module>` (see global rules) |
| `pyproject.toml` | Python — uv/poetry per project |
| `package.json` | Node/TypeScript |
| `Cargo.toml` | Rust |
| `ansible.cfg` + `playbooks/` | Ansible |

Active Go projects: `agentic-rag-mcp`, `go-versioning-helper`, `proto-store`, `provider`, `go-devtools`, `hit-blog`, `templates`, `gdt`, `rules_proto`.
Active TypeScript: `obsidian-tasks-system`, `obsidian-content-plugin`, `everything-claude-code`.
Active Python: `cv`. Active Ansible: `devbox-setup`. Active Rust: `rust-playground`, `rust-rag`.

## What not to do at this level

- Do not run `/init` against this directory expecting a project-style answer — re-run it inside the specific project.
- Do not write code, configs, scripts, or `.claude/` content at `<HOME>/Projects/`.
- Do not assume tooling from one project applies to another (e.g. `agentic-rag-mcp`'s Makefile targets do not exist in `provider/`).
- `projects.tar.bz2` at the root is a snapshot archive; leave it alone unless explicitly asked.

## Cross-cutting rules

The user's global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (User Authority Protocol, approval gates, language policy, Go formatting policy, agent workflow opt-in) applies everywhere in this workspace and overrides any project-level convention that conflicts with it.
